title: combretum collinum subsp. elgonense
intro: Combretum collinum subsp. elgonense, also known as Elgon bushwillow, is a plant species in the Combretaceae family, native to East Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to combat oxidative stress and promote wound healing. In traditional medicine, Combretum collinum subsp. elgonense is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to treat various ailments, and it is also used in folk medicine to treat skin conditions and promote digestive health.
